Avant d'ingérer un nouveau document, cha: Before ingesting a new document, each chunk is compared semantically to the already indexed chunks. If similarity exceeds the threshold, the chunk is ignored (quasi-identical duplicate).
Si décoché, les chunks sont ingérés sans: If unchecked, chunks are ingested without FAISS verification.\nUseful to force re-ingestion of a modified document.
Seuil cosinus [0.50 – 0.99] au-dessus du:
Cosine threshold [0.50 – 0.99] above which a chunk is
considered a semantic duplicate and ignored during ingest.

Example values:
  0.93 (default) — quasi-identical (same text slightly reformatted)
  0.85          — stylistic variants of the same passage
  0.70          — similar themes (risk of false positives)

↓ Reduce = more aggressive filtering (excludes more)
↑ Increase = stricter filtering (tolerates more variants)
💡 Astuce : augmentez le seuil (0.95–0.98: 💡 Tip: increase the threshold (0.95–0.98) if similar but distinct passages are incorrectly filtered. Reduce it (0.85–0.90) to eliminate close reformulations.

<style>\n body { margin: 0; padding: 0:
Special Commands

Certain prefixes at the beginning of a message trigger extended behaviors.

/web — Online Search

Prefix a message to have EUGENIA search the Web before answering. Useful to verify recent info, find references, or complete factual sections.
Example: /web German romanticism influence on Victor Hugo

/mem — Memory Search

Forces a search in the project's memory (notes, characters, places, already written scenes) before answering. Use this when looking for information you already gave to EUGENIA.
Example: /mem color of Alma's eyes

/edit — Co-author Editing Mode

Opens a new document in editing mode. EUGENIA becomes a co-author: her responses will modify the document directly. Use the editor's micro-command bar for quick instructions (shorten, make more lyrical, correct, expand).
Example: /edit Novel Prologue

/edition — Reopen Edited Document

Reloads an existing document in editing mode, identified by its exact title. The list of edited documents is also accessible in the Sources panel (double-click to reopen).
Example: /edition Novel Prologue

/stat — Custom Statistic

Asks EUGENIA to generate a custom statistic from the information you provide in the message or conversation context. The result is saved in the Statistics panel (left sidebar) and can be plotted (double-click the entry).
EUGENIA automatically selects the most suitable chart type: pie chart for proportions, bar chart for comparisons, line chart for evolutions over time.
Examples:
/stat make a pie chart of my family's social classes: 40% peasants, 30% nobles, 20% artisans, 10% clergy
/stat compare the sales of my three novels: novel A 1200 copies, novel B 850 copies, novel C 2100 copies

Natural Memorization

EUGENIA now has autonomous memory. If you tell her "memorize this" or "remember this information", she will use a secret internal tag to permanently archive the data in her relational memory.

You don't need to do anything special: when she triggers this action, a subtle bubble Information memorized: ... will appear in gray in the conversation to confirm it's written.

Other commands will be added here as development progresses.
Sensibilité du suivi scroll des annotati: Sensitivity of Ghost Writer annotations scroll tracking.\n1 = very slow, 5 = normal, 10 = very fast.

Sensitivity of Ghost Writer annotations scroll tracking.
1 = very slow, 5 = normal, 10 = very fast.
Distance entre les annotations Ghost Wri: Distance between Ghost Writer annotations and the right edge (px).
Windows OCR : rapide (~0.1s), natif Wind: Windows OCR: fast (~0.1s), native Windows 10/11.\nEasyOCR: precise but slow (3–8s, requires PyTorch).

Windows OCR: fast (~0.1s), native Windows 10/11.
EasyOCR: precise but slow (3–8s, requires PyTorch).
